dev_plans.content: LIGHTWEIGHT STRATIFIED NUMPY/SKLEARN POINT ESTIMATE. What is measured: the equal-opportunity recall gap for the DEATH class across 5 ethnicity buckets (white, black, hispanic, asian, other) at the HARD-frozen decision threshold 0.5. Concretely: for each ethnicity g, compute TPR_g = TP_g / (TP_g + FN_g) on the validation set at prediction > 0.5, then report max_g(TPR_g) - min_g(TPR_g). Where inserted: once per epoch, after the validation forward pass, inside the eval callback (NOT inside train.py's optimization loop — probe reads logits and targets only). Step-by-step: (1) After each epoch, run model.eval() on the val loader and collect (y_true, y_prob, ethnicity_onehot). (2) Binarize y_prob >= 0.5 to y_hat. (3) Build a dict mapping each ethnicity id -> boolean mask over rows. (4) For each group g, compute TP = ((y_hat==1) & (y_true==1) & mask).sum(), FN = ((y_hat==0) & (y_true==1) & mask).sum(), TPR_g = TP/(TP+FN), guarding against the degenerate case TP+FN==0 by setting TPR_g = NaN and skipping that group for the epoch (log a warning if >1 group is empty). (5) metric = nanmax(TPRs) - nanmin(TPRs). (6) Log to the epoch JSON alongside train_loss/val_auroc. Artifact: one scalar gap per epoch + a CSV table per epoch of per-ethnicity {count, TP, FN, TPR}. Alert is raised externally by orchestrator comparing gap to thresholds. No threshold tuning, no per-group offset, no re-sampling to correct the gap — the probe is purely observational.
dev_plans.metric: max_ethnicity(TPR@0.5) - min_ethnicity(TPR@0.5) for DEATH class per epoch, point estimate
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — the probe spec itself declares <0.05 as 'healthy parity within sampling noise'; this is the upper edge of acceptable noise on ~2k-val MIMIC splits and matches what careful ICU-mortality baselines report when no shortcut is present
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — the probe spec's upper boundary between healthy and unhealthy; a run landing here has detectable but not catastrophic disparity and usually cannot be reduced further without explicit fairness constraints, so it is the floor to stop the fix-loop from chasing

dev_plans.content: STATISTICALLY RIGOROUS BOOTSTRAP-INFERENCE APPROACH. What is measured: the upper bound of a 95 percentile-bootstrap confidence interval on the same max-min TPR gap as Plan 1. This is more conservative than the raw point estimate — it accounts for the fact that small ethnicity subgroups (asian, other, hispanic) have noisy TPR estimates and a single epoch's point estimate can look deceptively small by chance. Where inserted: once per epoch, after the validation forward pass, as a post-hoc statistical inference step on top of the per-epoch (y_true, y_prob, ethnicity) triple. Step-by-step: (1) Collect the same per-epoch validation predictions as Plan 1. (2) Compute the point-estimate gap g = max(TPR_g) - min(TPR_g). (3) Run B=2000 stratified bootstrap resamples over the val set rows (resampling within each ethnicity-stratum to preserve subgroup counts). For each resample b, recompute g_b. (4) Report the 95th-percentile of the bootstrap distribution as metric. (5) Also log: point estimate, the bootstrap standard error, and per-ethnicity 95% CIs on TPR for audit. (6) Drop a parquet artifact per epoch {epoch, point, lower95, upper95, se, per_group_csv}. Why this matters: on MIMIC's skewed ethnicity distribution, a raw gap <0.05 in a lucky epoch can still be statistically indistinguishable from a true gap of 0.12; the upper CI bound exposes that. The metric is conservative-by-design — it is the value the orchestrator should PASS/FAIL on, not the optimistic point estimate. No threshold tuning, no per-group offsets. Groups with <30 positive samples are flagged but still included (the bootstrap naturally widens their CI).
dev_plans.metric: 95th percentile of 2000 stratified-bootstrap resamples of max-min TPR@0.5 gap across 5 ethnicities per epoch
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.08 — slightly looser than Plan 1's 0.05 because the metric is an upper CI bound rather than a point estimate; a bootstrap-upper of 0.08 corresponds roughly to a true gap of ~0.05, matching the parity-within-noise criterion from the probe spec
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.13 — upper-CI-bound analogue of the probe's 0.10 unhealthy boundary; once the upper CI is at or above this the disparity is statistically undeniable and further pushes on the fix-loop have diminishing returns

dev_plans.content: TOOLING / FAIRLEARN LIBRARY FRAMEWORK. What is measured: the same equal-opportunity recall gap, but computed via the fairlearn.metrics MetricFrame infrastructure so that (a) the computation is auditable/reproducible via a well-tested library, (b) per-group breakdowns and group-pair tables are produced for free, and (c) the probe can be trivially extended later to equalized-odds (adding FPR gap) or calibration checks without re-writing code. Where inserted: once per epoch inside the eval callback, invoked between the validation forward pass and the train.py logging step. Step-by-step: (1) pip install fairlearn==0.12+ and import from fairlearn.metrics import MetricFrame. (2) After each epoch, gather y_true, y_pred_bin = (y_prob >= 0.5), and sensitive_features = ethnicity_labels (one of 'white','black','hispanic','asian','other'; 'other'/'unknown' merged into a single bucket — document the merge rule in a sidecar metadata JSON). (3) Build MetricFrame(metrics={'tpr': true_positive_rate_from_sklearn}, y_true=y_true, y_pred=y_pred_bin, sensitive_features=sensitive_features). (4) metric = mf.difference(method='between_groups') — this is exactly max-min per-group TPR but computed via the library path. (5) Also write mf.by_group.to_csv() and mf.group_metric_pairs_to_csv() per epoch to the probe artifacts folder. (6) Threshold 0.5 is enforced by the binarization above — do NOT call mf with y_prob and do NOT supply a decision-threshold search. The spec forbids threshold tuning. (7) Add a sanity check: assert that len(mf.by_group) == 5; if any ethnicity is missing from the val batch, log a warning and pad with NaN rather than silently dropping the group. Artifact per epoch: {gap_scalar, by_group_csv, pair_table_csv, metadata_json documenting merge rules}. The orchestrator reads only the scalar gap for PASS/FAIL; the per-group CSVs are audit trail for the mimic-race-fairness-recall-gap memory entry and for detecting whether the fix-loop is gaming the metric by collapsing a specific group's TPR.
dev_plans.metric: fairlearn MetricFrame(true_positive_rate).difference(method='between_groups') across 5 ethnicity buckets at threshold 0.5 per epoch
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — fairlearn's difference() computes the same max-min TPR gap as Plan 1, so the parity-within-noise calibration carries over directly; fairlearn's audited implementation guards against arithmetic edge cases that a hand-rolled numpy version might miss
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — same floor as Plan 1; on MIMIC mortality with this ethnicity distribution, empirical runs plateau near ~0.08–0.12 even with explicit fairness constraints, so 0.10 is the realistic stopping line before the fix-loop chases an unreachable standard
